rationale: The cited paper is referenced in multiple functionally important places, not just as a background list citation. It is used to position what prior MCS research has covered versus what this study adds, and it directly informs key conceptual development around modularity in the hypotheses. The authors also adapt their modularity measure from the cited study, indicating methodological dependence in construct operationalization. These roles make the citation substantively foundational to the citing paper's framing and model setup.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Ghosh, Dutta, and Stremersch (2006) as a core prior study in multicomponent-system marketing, drawing on it to frame the literature gap and to define the modularity construct in its theory. It also relies on that work to justify hypothesis logic about modularity-monitoring links and to adapt the modularity measurement scale.
